company_type: Electronic Design Automation (EDA) and Semiconductor IP Software

supply_chain.dependencies: Sole supplier for certain hardware components; reliance on third-party manufacturers for hardware products (emulation and prototyping systems); dependence on foundries for semiconductor manufacturing processes
supply_chain.risks: Delays, increased costs or quality issues from reliance on third parties to manufacture hardware products; sole supplier risk for certain hardware components; global semiconductor shortages; supply chain disruptions from sustained global inflationary pressures and elevated interest rates; geopolitical pressures affecting supply chain

regulatory_risks: U.S. Export Administration Regulations and export control restrictions
Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) Entity List restrictions
Export controls on advanced computing ICs and semiconductor manufacturing items
Export controls on transactions involving supercomputer and semiconductor manufacturing end-users
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
Anti-corruption laws in multiple jurisdictions
Privacy and data protection regulations (including EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive)
SEC climate-related reporting requirements
California climate-related disclosure laws
Tax law changes and interpretations (including Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, Inflation Reduction Act)
Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax (CAMT)
1% excise tax on stock repurchases (Inflation Reduction Act)
OECD Pillar Two Model Rules (15% global minimum tax)
Capitalization of research and development expenditures (Tax Cuts and Jobs Act)
California SB-167 and SB-175 (net operating loss and research credit limitations)
Customs and import requirements
Tariffs and trade barriers
ISO 26262 and ISO 21434 standards (automotive market)
Cybersecurity and data security regulations
Intellectual property laws and patent regulations
Antitrust and foreign investment regime clearances (for Ansys Merger)
Changes in accounting standards

sanction_risks: U.S. and Chinese trade regulations and export restrictions
Entity List restrictions affecting transactions with certain Chinese entities
Sanctions and financial penalties on participants in regional or global military conflicts
Government trade restrictions including tariffs, export controls and trade barriers
Regional or global military conflicts (Ukraine, Middle East)
China's policy of becoming global leader in semiconductor industry leading to potential trade disruptions
